title: Shared data channel design
abstract: Systems, methods and instrumentalities are disclosed for decoding data. For example, it may be determined, in a current slot, whether data received in a previous slot is decoded successfully. The data received in the previous slot may be included in a Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH). If the data received in the previous slot is not decoded successfully, preemptive multiplexing information may be detected in a first search space. The data received in the previous slot may be decoded, for example, using detected preemptive multiplexing information. The preemptive multiplexing information may be of a current slot. The preemptive multiplexing information may be comprised in a first DCI. A second search space of the current slot may be searched. For example, the second search space may be searched for a second DCI. The first DCI and the second DCI may be different.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is Claimed: 1 . A wireless transmit/receive unit (WTRU), comprising: a processor configured to: determine, in a current slot, whether data received in a previous slot is decoded successfully; if the data received in the previous slot is not decoded successfully: detecting, in the current slot, preemptive multiplexing information in a first search space, wherein the preemptive multiplexing information is comprised in a first DCI; and searching for a second DCI in a second search space of the current slot, wherein the second search space is different than the first search space. 2. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the data received in the previous slot is comprised in a Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH). 3. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the processor is further configured to decode the data received in the previous slot using the detected preemptive multiplexing information. 4. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the first search space of the current slot and the second search space of the current slot is searched via a radio network temporary identifier 2 (RNTI2). 5. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the processor is further configured to: monitor, in the current slot, a new radio physical downlink shared channel (NR-PDCCH) candidate from a search space, if the data received in the previous slot is not decoded successfully. 6. The WTRU of claim 5, wherein monitoring the NR-PDCCH candidate comprises monitoring resources configured by a higher layer signaling for enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB) and ultra-reliable low latency (URLLC) multiplexing. 7. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the first DCI and the second DCI are different. 8. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein at least one of a search space for ultra-reliable low latency (URLLC) and enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB) preemptive multiplexing, a WTRU specific search space, and a common search space is searched. 9. A method associated with decoding data, the method comprising: determining, in a current slot, whether data received in a previous slot is decoded successfully; if the data received in the previous slot is not decoded successfully: detecting preemptive multiplexing information in a first search space in the current slot, wherein the preemptive multiplexing information is comprised in a first DCI; and searching for a second DCI in a second search space of the current slot, wherein the second search space is different than the first search space. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the data received in the previous slot is comprised in a Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH). 1 1. The method of claim 9, further comprising decoding the data received in the previous slot using the detected preemptive multiplexing information. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein the first search space in the current slot and the second search space of the current slot is searched via a radio network temporary identifier 2 (RNTI2). 13. The method of claim 9, further comprising: if the data received in the previous slot is not decoded successfully: monitoring, in the current slot, a new radio physical downlink shared channel (NR-PDCCH) candidate from a search space. 14. The method of claim 9, wherein the first DCI and the second DCI are different. 15. The method of claim 9, wherein at least one of a search space for ultra-reliable low latency (URLLC) and enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB) preemptive multiplexing, a WTRU specific search space, and a common search space is searched.
